transcript.clean_text: foreign ever since the root of this year's Welter was announced stage six loomed large a big climbing day have a lambra the last visit we made here was in 2019 and we had great drama then a breakaway made it home and that was the prediction on this day but nobody nobody could really guess that this would be such a huge break that went for it in the end 40 Riders found themselves up the road not before quite a few bumps and bruises on route to our destination a couple of intermediate climbs along the way and then we were playing with the valley roads skirting mountains before the intermediate Sprint and a descent to the big climb itself well such was the pace in the battle to get in the break it took many prisoners none more so than Jay Vine who crashed out of the race unfortunately illness also being a factor in this race sadly but with 85.1 kilometers to go the break had a margin of around six minutes Evan Nepal said he wanted to hand over the jersey for a few days but he didn't think it would be this margin and those who are up front included some spectacular Rivals for the general classification jumbo vizma themselves had planted sepke televalta Dylan Van Baal up front intermediate Sprint points came about a seconds went to Solaire not that that would matter by the end of the day the margins were going to be much bigger the big climb was Julie arriving and when it did both the Peloton that was chasing and now smaller than the Breakaway itself would start to fracture evident Paul in the red leaders jersey giving Chase but in control of the day who got the Breakaway did fracture Rubio pushed on bardet was tempted to go for it Lenny matinees only 17 seconds down in the overall tucked in on barday's Wheel and set cus well he set off for Glory just a cherry on the cake you might say on a day that was bound to be good for yombo visma four up front roglitch giving Chase from the pack he was joined by Jana's finger girl they pushed on putting the hurt on Remco evidence pole at this point just could not manage to drive into the Gap sebkus was gunning for the stage win but would it be enough for the leader's Jersey Lenny Martinez was riding with barday some friction between the pair of them was reported by the time they crossed the line but emotions they rise up very quickly in situations such as this Martinez pushed on trying to Sprint home and grab the leader's Jersey attempting to become the youngest Rider since 1904. to wear a grand tour Leader's Jersey s came around the hoarded section took some high fives shed a few seconds there got himself 10 bonus seconds as well but was not to lead the race by the end of the day that would be the youngster Lenny Martinez Superstar of cycling already at the age of 20. brilliant about eight in third place and then we were starting to look for the gaps roglitch came to the line aided on occasion by vingego and then the clock ticked back where on Earth was Evan apoll in the end he was debris server lead over roglitch and vingego overall and hold on to Ninth Place in the general classification taking a fine win yombo visma taking a big stride towards glory in this race but Lenny Martinez taking control of the lead big emotions on a big climb is that curse couldn't look happier when he crossed the line great win 26 seconds in front of Martinez but the difference between them at the beginning of the day told at the end latiness is our new leader a star in the firmament group armor of course say goodbye to Pino hello to a raft of youngsters of which he is prime eight seconds margin he has overcuts ever Nepal still with a margin over both roglitch and Viggo this race is going all the way tomorrow it's downhill destination Oliver almost guaranteed to be one for the sprinters Caden Groves will start as favorite but then of course we have a very very busy weekend of climbing and who knows who will be in charge of this race by the first rest day race
